retry.notes: The ant's head and body must stay REDDISH-BROWN like the light fill — an ant is red-brown, never green. Keep the night palette dark but keep the subject's identity color.

night.review: The shipped 2026-07 night fill re-rolled the ant GREEN (the light fill is a proper reddish-brown ant) — a wrong subject-identity color, not a hue nuance. No gate checks subject-color plausibility (ISSUES Tier 1); compare against the light fill before accepting a take.
night.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the light↔night palette-coherence gap (ISSUES) shipping a real defect; all gates pass the green take.

retry.notes: Keep the pupils exactly as the pen draws them — outlined rings with a small solid white catchlight — and never fill a pupil with solid ink; solid chalk ink renders as glowing white in dark mode.

chalk.review: The pen has healthy RINGED pupils (light fill fully lively, 2/2 cores), but the shipped 2026-07 chalk solidified the eye interiors, so the composited night eyes read washed-out white with no legible pupil (ISSUES Tier 1). Judge on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE; after a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the ISSUES chalk-whitening class introduced by the chalk itself on a healthy ringed pen; every gate passes the defective take.

flags.notes: THE EYES ARE THE STAR OF THIS PAGE. The spider's body is dark, but every eye must stay lively: light sclera, deep near-black pupil, bright white catchlight. Do NOT flood the eyes with the body color or navy.

night.why: Durable subject quirk: the dark-bodied spider tempts the model to flood the eyes — burned ~26 attempts before this note was discovered (commit d96ae6f; pipeline.md Stage 4 lever 4). Note text reconstructed from that prose. Re-validated on gemini-3.1: passed every gate FIRST take with this note auto-applied (ideas-exploration/idea-10).

flags.notes: Paint every shell circle DEEP NEAR-BLACK — the ladybug's spots are black, never white or light.

night.why: Canon fact, durable across models: shell spots are black (pipeline.md Stage 4 lever 4; regen in d96ae6f after a per-eye relaxation shipped a dead-sclera ladybug).

flags.notes: The ladybug's shell spots are BLACK in canon — never fill a shell circle white. Whiten only the eye sclera and catchlights.

chalk.why: Canon fact: the 2.5-era chalk's one bad judgment call was white shell spots (commit e99fd22, fixed with this note at low temperature). The 3.1 wave chalked the page clean without it, so the paired -t 0.2 was dropped as a dead-model workaround; the note stays because the misfire is a judgment call no gate can see.

normalize.why: The faithful de-swirl took t 0.2 plus this note (commit 551ab52; pipeline.md Stage 1 gate 6 — the first redraw produced hypno-swirl eyes). 2.5-era values kept as the starting point: the normalizer has not been exercised on gemini-3.1 yet (ISSUES).

retry.notes: Draw each catchlight as a SMALL SOLID WHITE DOT inside the pupil, never as an outlined hollow circle, and keep exactly the pen's one-ring-one-catchlight eye structure.

chalk.review: The pen has healthy RINGED pupils (light fill fully lively, 6/6 cores), but the shipped 2026-07 chalk redrew the eye interiors with hollow outlined-circle catchlights that composite as empty navy rings floating in the eye (googly bubbles). Judge on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE (ISSUES Tier 1); after a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — the ISSUES chalk-whitening class introduced by the chalk itself on a healthy ringed pen; every gate passes the defective take.

flags.notes: Each eye must end as EXACTLY one thin pupil ring with one small catchlight circle inside it — redraw the small solid pupils as outlined rings; do not leave any solid ink in the eyes.

normalize.why: Small pupils under ~4px strokes ducked the fixed erosion radius; took an eye-specific note at t 0.7-0.8 (commit 6840bba; pipeline.md Stage 1 gate 6). Note text reconstructed. Normalizer not yet exercised on gemini-3.1 (ISSUES).

flags.notes: The sky has THREE CLOUDS — keep all three exactly where they are.

normalize.why: Durable motif guard: the first normalization silently DELETED a cloud (worst-tile keep 0% — pipeline.md Stage 1); the same deletion class hit objects/teddy-tall's star (idea-run #6).
